steps.title: Find the slope from two table points
Test each equation with a known point
Confirm with a second point
steps.content: Substitute (-6, 65) into A: 9(-6)+3(65)=-54+195=141 ✓. Only choice A is consistent with the table.

steps.title: Evaluate all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '9x+3y=141': Equivalent to 3x+y=47, slope = -3. Passes through (-6,65) and (6,29). ✓
  • Choice B — '9x+3y=3': Equivalent to 3x+y=1, y-intercept 1. Does not match the table. ✗
  • Choice C — '3x+9y=141': Equivalent to x+3y=47, slope = -1/3. Does not match slope -3. ✗
  • Choice D — '3x+9y=3': Equivalent to x+3y=1, slope = -1/3 and wrong intercept. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
